body.title: DS commits attribute Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6, but DS runs devstral-only
body.description:
DickSimnel's commit-message template hardcodes `Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>` in devices/dicksimnel/device.py:114 and devices/dicksimnel/toolloop.py:124. But DS's _TIER_CASCADE is OLLAMA-ONLY (devstral-small-2:24b, OR/paid tiers disabled) — it never runs Sonnet. Verified live on commit 165890f0 (T-web-queue-status-test-stale): $0 devstral run, commit said Sonnet 4.6. This is misleading provenance: the commit log claims a model that did not produce the work. Not a cost event (the run was genuinely free), purely an attribution defect. Also observed: git author surfaced as akien@example.com placeholder rather than a DS identity.

**Affected files:** devices/dicksimnel/device.py (line ~114, prompt/commit template), devices/dicksimnel/toolloop.py (line ~124, commit-message instruction). Optionally DS git author/email config.
**Design rules:** none apply (string/template change); provenance-accuracy is the intent.
**Scope boundary:** IN - change the hardcoded co-author to truthfully reflect DS/devstral (e.g. `Co-Authored-By: DickSimnel (devstral-small-2:24b) <noreply@anthropic.com>` or an agreed DS identity). OUT - changing the tier cascade, the commit flow, or CC's own co-author convention.
**Test plan:** no automated test (template string); manual confirm via a follow-up DS dispatch that the next DS commit footer reflects the corrected attribution. Alternatively grep the templates assert the Sonnet-4.6 string is gone.
body.result: CC-validated: grep clean, both template sites corrected
